encounter.reason: Acute perforated appendicitis with periappendiceal abscess
encounter.location: Nebraska Medicine, Operating Room 7, 4350 Dewey Avenue, Omaha, NE 68105

conditions.name: acute gangrenous appendicitis with perforation
conditions.note: Preoperative diagnosis of acute perforated appendicitis established during preceding ED encounter based on CT findings. Operative findings refined diagnosis — appendix visually confirmed gangrenous with transmural involvement and visible perforation at distal tip.

conditions.name: periappendiceal abscess
conditions.note: Initially identified on CT during ED encounter as 2.3 cm rim-enhancing fluid collection. Operatively confirmed as 2.5 cm abscess cavity adjacent to cecum base containing approximately 30 mL purulent fluid. Abscess drained and irrigated intraoperatively.

medications.name: piperacillin-tazobactam 3.375 g IV
medications.sig: piperacillin-tazobactam 3.375 g intravenous, once, administered at 18:50 on 2026-01-12 as pre-operative antibiotic prophylaxis
medications.reason: pre-operative antibiotic prophylaxis for perforated appendicitis

medications.name: piperacillin-tazobactam 3.375 g IV every 6 hours
medications.reason: post-operative antibiotic therapy for perforated appendicitis with periappendiceal abscess

medications.name: acetaminophen 1000 mg IV every 6 hours
medications.sig: acetaminophen 1000 mg intravenous every 6 hours, scheduled

medications.name: morphine sulfate 2-4 mg IV every 4 hours PRN
medications.sig: morphine sulfate 2 to 4 mg intravenous every 4 hours as needed for pain

medications.name: heparin 5,000 units subcutaneous every 8 hours
medications.sig: heparin 5,000 units subcutaneous injection every 8 hours

procedures.name: laparoscopic appendectomy
procedures.technique: three-port laparoscopic approach (umbilical 12 mm, suprapubic 5 mm, left lower quadrant 5 mm); Veress needle insufflation to 15 mmHg; mesoappendix divided with vascular endoscopic stapler; appendiceal base secured and transected with endoscopic stapler; specimen removed in endoscopic retrieval bag through umbilical port

procedures.name: drainage of periappendiceal abscess
procedures.body_site: right lower quadrant, periappendiceal space adjacent to cecum base
procedures.description: aspiration of approximately 30 mL purulent fluid from right paracolic gutter; thorough irrigation of abscess cavity with 500 mL warm normal saline until effluent returned clear

procedures.name: placement of Jackson-Pratt drain, right lower quadrant
procedures.description: 10-French Jackson-Pratt drain placed into the right lower quadrant through the right-sided 5 mm port site and secured to skin

document_references.description: Detailed operative report by Dr. Marcus Okonkwo documenting emergent laparoscopic appendectomy with abscess drainage and JP drain placement. Covers preoperative and postoperative diagnoses, three-port laparoscopic technique, operative findings of gangrenous perforated appendix with 2.5 cm periappendiceal abscess, 50 mL EBL, specimen disposition, drain placement, and post-operative orders.

Record of general endotracheal anesthesia for emergent laparoscopic appendectomy. Documents anesthesia induction, intraoperative monitoring, IV fluid administration, and patient recovery/transfer to PACU in stable condition.
Pre-operative evaluation documenting the patient's presenting symptoms, ED workup findings, relevant medical history, informed consent discussion, and surgical risk assessment.

Nursing documentation of intraoperative care including patient positioning, surgical prep, sponge/needle/instrument counts (correct x2), specimen handling and labeling, drain placement documentation, and patient transfer to PACU in stable condition.

diagnostic_reports.type: surgical pathology examination of appendix specimen
diagnostic_reports.description: Appendix specimen sent to surgical pathology (accession SP-2026-00412) at time of surgery. Specimen placed in endoscopic retrieval bag and removed through umbilical port site. Results not yet available; pathology report expected on subsequent date.
